title: Issue 1116 — GPS track overlays & tag-based map layer controls

questions.prompt: What is in scope for this goal?
questions.description: Everything below is recommended IN. Deselect anything you want to defer. KML import is explicitly deferred.
questions.recommendedAnswer: All of: track overlay type, tags on all four overlay types, native Leaflet layer control, server-side GetTrackGeometry RPC, lazy track loading, widget upload affordance, agent-mediated upload via MCP, track download link, help docs.

options.label: New GPS track overlay type (file-referenced, not in frontmatter)
Tags on markers, polygons, circles, AND tracks
Native Leaflet layer control to show/hide by tag
Lazy/progressive track geometry loading in the widget
Hover-revealed track upload in the widget (first FE mutation surface)
Track download link from the widget
Help documentation for all of the above

questions.prompt: Which track file formats should the first implementation accept?
questions.description: Rever and Garmin both export GPX (track trk/trkseg and route rte variants). GeoJSON is trivial to parse server-side. KML is deferred per the issue.
questions.recommendedAnswer: GPX (primary, Rever/Garmin) and GeoJSON. KML deferred.

options.label: KML now (NOT recommended — issue defers it)

questions.prompt: What fields should the widget's quick-upload popover expose?
questions.description: The agent path always exposes full config (color, popup, tags, sort order). This is about the in-widget quick path only.
questions.recommendedAnswer: File + label (defaults to filename) + optional comma-separated tags.

options.label: File + label only (tag later via agent)

questions.prompt: How should very large tracks be handled so they stay renderable?
questions.description: A long ADV GPX can have tens of thousands of points. Simplifying server-side keeps the initial render fast.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Simplify server-side (Douglas–Peucker) down to a point ceiling (e.g. ~5000) before returning geometry.

options.label: Simplify server-side to a point ceiling (recommended)

questions.prompt: When an overlay carries multiple tags, when is it visible?
questions.description: Drives how the layer control filters overlays that belong to more than one tag group.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Visible if ANY of its tags is enabled (OR). Untagged overlays are always visible.

options.label: Visible if ANY enabled tag matches (OR) — recommended
Visible only if ALL its tags are enabled (AND)
